summary: Basic mobile FPS weapon feel now includes faster movement, wall-shot detection, double-tap-safe fire input, weapon-aware wall clearance, wider structural entries, and shared shot-effect pose alignment.
observations: The right-side fire button calls the weapon system without stealing aim drags elsewhere on the look zone.
SPARK, BORE, and VAULT use different cadence and recoil values.
Ammo and reload state are exposed in the HUD and debug API.
Player movement speed is now 4.0625 units per second.
Last shot debug state reported a wall hit at tile [22, 1] from spawn-facing fire smoke.
Player collision includes a manifest-derived right-handed weapon footprint.
WeaponSystem exposes wallAvoidance in the weapon debug snapshot.
Right-shifted viewmodels reduce overlap with the bottom-center weapon tray.
Structural wall-band entries now require 5 open tiles so the weapon footprint does not make door-like breaks feel like 2 usable units.
Shot feedback uses a shared weapon view pose so tracer start follows active weapon offset, recoil, and wall retraction.
decisions: Keep one fire button for now.
Use Space and number keys only as desktop smoke-test helpers.
Keep procedural audio until ElevenLabs assets are intentionally generated.
Keep shot wall feedback lightweight until proper tracer and impact assets are sourced.
Use viewmodel retraction for rotation-near-wall cases that movement collision cannot solve alone.
Keep structural wall-band entries wider than the ordinary 3-tile traversal lane minimum.
caughtIssues: Fast repeated fire needed explicit double-tap zoom prevention.
Movement-only collision cannot prevent weapon clipping when the player rotates in place beside a wall.
risks: Without hit detection or enemy response, weapon feel can only be judged on cadence, recoil, audio, and UI responsiveness.
Weapon footprint clearance can make tight turns feel heavier if future maps shrink below the 3-tile lane rule.
Door-like entries below 5 tiles can feel narrower than their symbol-map width once the carried weapon is included.
